question_text: During summer, why are the days longer than the nights?

options.label: A
options.text: The Sun moves faster across the sky in summer

options.label: B
options.text: Your hemisphere is tilted towards the Sun, so it stays in daylight for longer

options.label: C
options.text: The Earth spins faster in summer

options.label: D
options.text: There are more hours in a summer day

explanation: When your hemisphere is tilted towards the Sun, it spends more time facing the Sun as Earth rotates, creating longer daylight hours. This is because of the tilt of the Earth's axis.
